---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
##library(readxl)
##library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

background-image: url('libs/Images/00-Leviathan_Cover_55.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size70[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size60[.center[
Case Studies:

Can terrorism succeed?
]]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Fall 2023)
]]

???

### Prep for Class
1. Review submitted cases
    - https://docs.google.com/document/d/1VxpQcFCyLm9UySFbvhUDyl9pe0c-C3P4VeOAjqGGN2U/edit?usp=sharing

<br>

We're tackling a big question this week: Does terrorism work?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-red.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center, inverse, textwhite

.size55[**Bring to class a piece of evidence showing terrorism (e.g. a terrorist group, a terrorist threat or a specific terror attack) changing the behavior of a target audience (e.g. society, community, government) in some specific way.**]

???

### Everybody ready to go on this?

<br>

### All citations in APA format?




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-red.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center, inverse, textwhite

.size65[**Under what conditions are terrorists more or less likely to succeed in their aims?**]

???

Our goal for today is to answer this question.

### What does the framing of this question indicate about how I'm setting up our exploration?

- Assumes strategic behavior by instrumentally rational actors, e.g. those with goals
- Assumes we can identify "success" vs "failure"
- ?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-red.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, inverse, textwhite

.size55[.center[**Under what conditions are terrorists more or less likely to succeed in their aims?**]

1. Criteria raising P(Success)?

2. Criteria lowering P(Success)?
]

???

To start, everyone take five minutes to brainstorm these two lists on your own.

- Don't just root this in your case for today, draw on your intuitions and the work we've done over the last few weeks.

- Our cases today will then allow us to test these hypotheses.

<br>

Small groups: Compare and consolidate lists

<br>

Class: Let's build our lists on the board



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-red.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, inverse, textwhite

.size50[
1. Describe your case
    - Who is the "terrorist"?
    - What was their "goal"?
    - What did they do?
    - Was this "change" a "success"?

2. Apply our criteria to your case
]

???

Now it's time for everyone to introduce their case AND use it as a test of our criteria.

Take a moment to get ready.

<br>

Ok, let's hear our cases and evaluate our criteria!




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/13-1-belfast_mural.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: center

.size45[.content-box-red[**How and when can terrorism succeed?**]]

???

<br>

Where do we end up? 

### How and when does terrorism succeed?

<br>

### - Was this a good test of our hypotheses? Why or why not?

### - Are we comfortable saying that impact equals effectiveness? Why or why not?

### - What does our sample selection need to improve this test of our criteria?
